question_text: Let a random sample of size 4 from a normal population with unknown mean 𝜇

options.A: nd variance 1 yield the sample mean of 0.16. Let Φ(⋅) be the cumulative
options.D: istribution function of the standard normal random variable. It is given that Φ(2.28) = 0.989, Φ(1.96) = 0.975, Φ(1.64) = 0.95. If the likelihood ratio test of size 0.05 is used to test 𝐻0: 𝜇= 0 against 𝐻1: 𝜇≠0, then the power of the test at the sample mean equals __________ (rounded off to three decimal places).
